invariant: A claim with temporal_scope = closed (the original observation's window has ended) cannot be re-corroborated by data observed after the closure. temporal_freshness factor must penalize attempts to refresh closed-scope claims with stale or post-closure evidence; the closed claim retains its original trust_score band rather than getting refreshed by inappropriate evidence.
pass_fail_definition: PASS = the closed-scope claim's trust_score does NOT increase when post-closure evidence arrives AND the freshness_weight ConfidenceEvidence reflects the temporal_scope penalty. FAIL = trust_score increases as if the claim were still open-scope (substrate ignored temporal_scope).

fixture_design_notes.state_sql: Seed 1 account (acme.example.com) with 1 prior claim X committed at T-180d with temporal_scope = closed and observed_at_window_end = T-180d. The claim's original trust_score is recorded.
fixture_design_notes.provider_replay: IntelligenceJson at T0 (clock.txt) returns content that purports to corroborate X with new data observed at T-30d (i.e., 150d after the closed-scope window ended).
fixture_design_notes.expected_state: After commit_claim path: the closed claim X's trust_score is unchanged (no inappropriate refresh). A new corroboration row is rejected at the temporal-scope gate, OR is added but does not affect the closed claim's score. freshness_weight ConfidenceEvidence on any new corroborated claim shows the closed-scope penalty.
fixture_design_notes.expected_provenance: Sources include both seeded sources with their observed_at timestamps. Field attribution shows the temporal_scope mismatch. Warnings include 'corroboration_outside_temporal_scope'.
fixture_design_notes.expected_output: Rendered output shows X as it was at closure; no inappropriate refresh.
